human_verification.notes: Reviewed manifest, scenario coverage, evaluation results, readiness review, deployment notes, and marketing notes for consistency with issue #147 and the repository delivery contract.

specialist_owned_evidence: Narrow specialty boundary is limited to legacy modernization planning, dependency discovery, sequencing, and escalation rather than implementation or operations execution.
Authority-source baseline uses real public vendor and standards sources focused on modernization planning, legacy discovery, coexistence, and interoperability.
Scenario suite includes planning, batch and middleware-aware transition risks, value sequencing, boundary rejection, and low-confidence escalation behavior.
delegated_meta_agent_evidence: Shared best-practice curation and authority-source normalization are delegated to #225 and #306 with a safe local baseline fallback.
Shared evaluation, readiness, deployment, commercialization, boundary, and scaffolding normalization are delegated to #302 through #308 with explicit fallback contracts.
The package can initialize and operate safely even when shared meta-agents are unavailable, stale, or low-confidence.
notes: Issue #147 specialist-owned responsibilities are encoded in manifest.yaml under shared_meta_agent_contracts.
validated claim is supported by complete required artifacts, threshold-passing evaluation results, human verification, deployment notes, and commercialization notes.
No unreviewed regressions were introduced and rollout safety is documented through rollback and monitoring expectations.
